name: Cranberry
description_short: Cranberry Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards to eligible members.
description_long:
Cranberry Federal Credit Union (Cranberry FCU) is a federally-chartered credit union operating under NCUA insurance (routing #211381327). As a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative, it serves individuals and families seeking alternatives to traditional for-profit banks with competitive rates and member-focused services.

Cranberry FCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings deposit accounts, vehicle loans, mortgages, home equity lines of credit (HELOC), personal loans, debt consolidation loans, credit cards, and college student loans. The credit union prominently advertises vehicle loan rates starting as low as 3.99% for up to 65 months (for model years 2022-2026, under 50,000 miles, with A+ credit of 730+ Vantage 4 Score or better). Members can apply online for most products and access online banking services.

The credit union distinguishes itself through member-focused pricing and community involvement. Cranberry FCU maintains multiple physical locations, offers Mass Save energy efficiency financing, provides Zelle money transfer integration, and actively participates in community sponsorships and scholarship programs. The organization invites membership on its Board of Directors, emphasizing member governance typical of credit union structures.

Cranberry FCU is most appropriate for consumers who qualify for membership and seek competitive loan rates, particularly for vehicle financing and mortgages. The main caveat is that credit union membership eligibility may be restricted by employment, residency, or other affiliation requirements (not detailed on the public website), which may limit access compared to banks open to all.
services: First mortgages and home purchase financing
Online banking and digital money transfer (Zelle)
Financial calculators and loan rate tools
pros: Vehicle loan rates as low as 3.99% for 65-month terms on qualifying vehicles (2022-2026 models, A+ credit)
Multiple physical branch locations for in-person service and member interaction
Member-owned cooperative structure with governance representation via Board of Directors
NCUA insurance protection on deposit accounts (equivalent to FDIC for credit unions)
Online banking and digital tools including Zelle money transfer integration
Community involvement including sponsorships and scholarship programs
Comprehensive product suite: checking, savings, mortgages, HELOCs, personal loans, and credit cards
cons: Membership eligibility not clearly disclosed on website—likely restricted by employment, residency, or other criteria
Advertised rates (3.99%) require A+ credit (730+ score) and vehicle conditions that may exclude many borrowers
Limited transparency on deposit account interest rates, APRs for credit cards, and personal loan terms visible on main website
No information provided about membership fees, minimum deposit requirements, or account maintenance costs
Website does not specify service area or geographic membership restrictions
best_for: Eligible members seeking competitive vehicle refinancing or new auto loans with established credit
Homeowners or home buyers seeking mortgage or HELOC products with credit union member pricing
Consumers already employed or affiliated with organizations that grant credit union membership eligibility
Community-minded individuals valuing member ownership and local credit union participation
diagnosis: Cranberry FCU is best for credit union-eligible members seeking competitive rates on auto loans, mortgages, and other consumer credit products, particularly those with good to excellent credit. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted (details not publicly disclosed) and advertised rates require A+ credit profiles, limiting access for many consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Cranberry — Credit Union in West Wareham, MA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Cranberry — NCUA-insured credit union in West Wareham, MA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
